Voyage Policy: Covers a specific voyage from one port to another.

Time Policy: Covers the insured for a specific period, regardless of the number of voyages.

Floating Policy: A single policy that covers multiple shipments over a period, rather than individual shipments.

Valued Policy: Specifies a fixed amount that will be paid in the event of a loss, regardless of the actual value of the goods at the time of loss.

Cost of goods sold is
explanation:
Cost of goods sold = opening stock + purchases - closing stock - returns outwards (returns outwards represent goods that were bought but returned to the supplier due to one reason or the other. It will be subtracted from the purchase)

10,000 + 280,000 - (8,000 - 3,000)

290,000 - 11,000 = 279,000
